conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances. Let me start by reading the official eligibility guidance.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. Now I can help assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To get started, could you please tell me: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Hello! Yes, I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit and would like to know if I'm eligible. 

I have one child - my daughter Alex, she's 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have one daughter, Alex, who is 8 years old.

Now I need to ask you a few questions to assess your eligibility:

**Do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Now I need to understand your living arrangements with Alex:

**Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, she lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for her.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One final question to complete my assessment:

**Is Alex currently in hospital for more than 12 weeks, or in care for more than 8 weeks?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, she's not in hospital or care at all. She's at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for answering all my questions. I now have all the information I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

Based on what you've told me, let me provide you with my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.**

You meet all the key eligibility requirements:
- You live in the UK
- Alex is under 16 years old (she's 8)
- You are responsible for Alex as she lives with you
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for her
- She is not in hospital or care

You can proceed with making a claim for Child Benefit for Alex. Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, meeting the age requirement. You live in the UK and Alex lives with you at home, which means you are responsible for her. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and she is not in hospital or care. You meet all the eligibility criteria: you are responsible for a child under 16, you live in the UK, the child lives with you, and no one else is claiming for the same child.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 8-year-old daughter Alex. Alex lives at home with the claimant, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for her, and she is not in hospital or care. The claimant meets all the standard eligibility requirements for Child Benefit.

No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and she is not in hospital or care. You meet all the eligibility criteria: you are responsible for a child under 16, you live in the UK, the child lives with you, and no one else is claiming for the same child.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 8-year-old daughter Alex. Alex lives at home with the claimant, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for her, and she is not in hospital or care. The claimant meets all the standard eligibility requirements for Child Benefit.
